title: DNS Resolution Flow
description: Step-by-step walkthrough of how a domain name is resolved to an IP address.
fullExplanation: DNS resolution is a hierarchical lookup process. When you type google.com, your browser checks multiple caches before making any network requests. If not cached, a recursive resolver (your ISP or 8.8.8.8) does the heavy lifting — querying root servers, TLD servers, and authoritative servers on your behalf. The entire process typically takes 20-120ms for a cold lookup, but cached responses are instant.

steps.name: Browser Cache
steps.description: Browser checks its own DNS cache. Chrome: chrome://net-internals/#dns. TTL-based expiry.

steps.name: OS Cache
steps.description: OS checks its DNS cache (nscd, systemd-resolved on Linux; DNS Client service on Windows).

steps.name: /etc/hosts
steps.description: OS checks local hosts file (/etc/hosts on Linux/Mac, C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts on Windows).

steps.name: Recursive Resolver
steps.description: Query sent to configured DNS server (ISP resolver, 8.8.8.8, 1.1.1.1). Resolver checks its own cache.

steps.name: Root Nameserver
steps.description: Resolver queries a root server (13 clusters: a-m.root-servers.net). Root returns TLD nameserver address.

steps.name: TLD Nameserver
steps.description: Resolver queries .com TLD server. Returns authoritative nameserver for google.com.

steps.name: Authoritative NS
steps.description: Resolver queries google.com's authoritative nameserver. Returns actual A/AAAA record.

steps.name: Response Cached
steps.description: Resolver caches response per TTL. Returns IP to client. Client caches per TTL.

keyFacts: 13 root server clusters (a-m.root-servers.net) — each is anycast with hundreds of nodes worldwide
Recursive resolver does all the work — client only talks to resolver
TTL (Time To Live): how long to cache the record. Low TTL = frequent lookups but faster propagation.
Negative caching: NXDOMAIN (non-existent domain) responses also cached
dig +trace google.com: shows full resolution chain step by step
nslookup / dig: tools to manually query DNS
DNS over HTTPS (DoH) / DNS over TLS (DoT): encrypt DNS queries for privacy
